ut1-3-1.title: Hands-on: Read the live A-scan on a stepped block
ut1-3-1.intro: Drag the 0-degree probe across the stepped block and watch the back-wall echo slide along the time base — horizontal position is time of flight, vertical height is returned energy. Log readings on three steps, then use the sweep alone to call the thinnest one.

tasks.label: Record A-scan readings on three different steps
tasks.hint: Center the probe on a step; the back-wall echo lands farther right on the sweep as the steel under it gets thicker.

tasks.label: Pick the step whose back-wall echo arrives earliest
tasks.hint: Least metal means shortest time of flight — choose the step whose echo sits closest to the initial pulse.

ut1-3-2.title: Hands-on: Peak the SDH echoes that set sensitivity
ut1-3-2.intro: A reference reflector only means something at its echo peak. Slide the angle-beam wedge over two side-drilled holes — the same known reflectors a procedure names for setting the reference level — and mark each one exactly where its A-scan echo maximizes.

tasks.label: Maximize and mark the first side-drilled hole
tasks.hint: Inch the wedge until the echo stops growing, then mark within 4 mm of peak — the same move as maximizing a cal reflector.

tasks.label: Maximize and mark the second side-drilled hole
tasks.hint: Keep sliding past the first hole; a fresh echo rises as the beam centreline approaches the next SDH.

ut1-3-3.title: Hands-on: Gauge a thin step wedge with T = vt/2
ut1-3-3.intro: A digital gauge just times the round trip and computes thickness as velocity times time over two. Work the probe across a thin-wall step wedge, record three readings, and flag the minimum wall — the number a corrosion survey exists to find.

tasks.label: Take gauge readings on three different steps
tasks.hint: Hold the probe flat on each step — the readout follows T = vt/2 from the timed back-wall echo.

tasks.label: Flag the thinnest step as the critical wall
tasks.hint: Sweep all four steps and compare numbers — the minimum wall is what the survey reports.
